title: blepharipappus scaber subsp. laevis
intro: Blepharipappus scaber subsp. laevis, also known as a subspecies of the rough-fruited fleabane, is a flowering plant native to western North America, characterized by its small, white to purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Blepharipappus scaber subsp. laevis include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
